phase2.errorMessage: (Compiles fine — but the logic is WRONG! The second Debug.Log always runs, regardless of the if condition.)

phase3.prompt:
Which of these is a correct if-else statement in C#?

A) `if (health > 50) { } else { }`
B) `if health > 50 { } else { }`
C) `if (health > 50) { } else`
D) `if health > 50 else { }`

Select the CORRECT answer.
dialogueHints: Think about the required syntax: parentheses around the condition.
The else block needs braces too if it has multiple statements.
phase3.openingDialogue: Syntax check! Which one is correct? 🤔
